en: Very passable, this, eh? Very passable. Right.
A good glass of Chateau de Chasseler, eh, Josiah?
Aye, you're right there, Robert Dyer.
Who would have thought, thirty years ago, we'd all be sitting here drinking Chateau de Chasseler, eh?
ko: 아, 이거 괜찮네. 꽤 괜찮아. 나쁘지 않아

en: Them days, we were glad to have the price of a cup of tea.
ko: 샤토 드 샤슬라 한 잔만큼 좋은 건 없지

en: Aye, a cup of cold tea. Without milk or sugar.
ko: 샤토 드 샤슬라 한 잔만큼 좋은 건 없지

en: Or tea. In a cracked cup and all.
ko: 드 사르야. 맞아, 로버트. 그래 맞아

en: Oh, we never used to have a cup. We used to have to drink out of a rolled up newspaper.
ko: 40년 전에 우리가 여기 앉아서 샤토 드 샤슬라를 마시게 될 줄 누가 알았겠어

en: The best we could manage was to suck on a piece of damp cloth.
ko: 그때는 차 한 잔 값도 감사했을 텐데. 그래. 차가운 차. 그래. 우유도 설탕도 없는. 그래

en: But, you know, we were happy in those days, although we were poor.
ko: 그때는 차 한 잔 값도 감사했을 텐데. 그래. 차가운 차. 그래. 우유도 설탕도 없는. 그래

en: Because we were poor? Aye. My old dad used to say to me, money doesn't bring you happiness, son.
ko: 차도 없었지. 그래. 우리는 컵도 없었어

en: He was right. Aye. I was happier then and I had nothing.
We used to live in this tiny old tumble-down house with great big holes in the roof.
House? You were lucky to live in a house. We used to live in one room.
ko: 가난했지만. 가난했으니까. 그래. 아버지가 그랬어, 돈이 행복을 가져다주진 않는다고
